name: Dehydrate after first read (happy path)
trigger: Assistant message reaches finish='stop' on a turn whose parent user message has image attachment_ref parts
preconditions: User message has 1+ attachment_ref parts of mime type image/*
All attachment_refs currently have dehydrated !== true (or undefined)

messages.description: Post-completion hook fires with assistant message id
Read parent user message + its attachment_ref parts
Read assistant response text
extractAnnotation(responseText) → trimmed string ≤ 4000 chars
For each image attachment_ref: read binary from attachments table
put(sessionID, filename, bytes) — write binary to ~/.local/state/opencode/incoming/<sid>/<filename>
Returns absolute path of staged file
Session.updatePart with dehydrated=true, annotation, sha256, incoming_path; delete binary row from attachments
Emit telemetry: attachment.dehydrated { sessionID, filename, sha256, originalEstTokens, annotationChars }

postconditions: Each image attachment_ref now has dehydrated=true with annotation populated
Session sqlite attachments table has one fewer binary row per dehydrated image
Next LLM call wire payload will contain <dehydrated_attachment> text block instead of image_url

name: Reread fetches binary back from staging
preconditions: Some prior turn has dehydrated an attachment with that filename
Binary file at ~/.local/state/opencode/incoming/<sid>/<filename> still exists (within TTL)

messages.description: Tool invocation with filename arg
get(sessionID, filename) → bytes | null
Returns binary bytes
Tool result: { type: 'image', url: 'data:image/png;base64,...', est_tokens, byte_size }
Emit telemetry: attachment.rereaded { sessionID, filename }

postconditions: Tool result is appended to conversation as a tool-result part containing the image
Next LLM call sees the image binary again (one-shot rehydration via tool channel)

name: Reread fails after GC
trigger: Model calls reread_attachment after the binary has been GC'd
preconditions: Session was deleted > TTL days ago

messages.description: Tool invocation
get(sessionID, filename) → null
Tool result: { error: 'attachment_expired', message: 'Image was staged but has been garbage-collected past its TTL.' }

postconditions: Model receives clear error message and can request re-upload from user

name: Subsequent LLM call serializes dehydrated stub
trigger: Next turn's LLM.stream prepares messages[] including a dehydrated attachment_ref part
preconditions: Conversation history contains at least one attachment_ref with dehydrated=true

messages.description: Read conversation messages
MessageV2.toModelMessages(messages, model)
For each part: if attachment_ref + dehydrated=true → emit text block <dehydrated_attachment filename='X' sha256='Y'>annotation</dehydrated_attachment>; else emit image_url block as today
Returns ModelMessage[] with dehydrated attachments as text blocks
Wire payload contains text annotation, NOT image binary; input_tokens drops accordingly

postconditions: Provider receives dramatically smaller payload for dehydrated attachments
Token usage drops from ~20K per image to ~200-1000 per annotation

name: GC sweep at daemon startup
trigger: Daemon starts up (cli/cmd/serve.ts handler initialization phase)
preconditions: ~/.local/state/opencode/incoming/ exists (or will be created on first put)

messages.description: Triggers gcSweep() on startup
list all sessionIDs that have an incoming dir
For each sid, look up Session.get(sid) → check if deleted > TTL days ago OR session doesn't exist
deleteSession(sid) for each eligible — recursive rm
Emit telemetry: attachment.gc.swept { sessionsScanned, sessionsDeleted, bytesFreed, durationMs }

postconditions: Disk usage in ~/.local/state/opencode/incoming/ reflects only sessions still active or within TTL grace window
